reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C–O and C–C bonds in isoxazole ring
bond_broken: N–H (tautomerization of ethyl nitroacetate to nitrile oxide), loss of H2O

feed_streams.reasoning: Both substrates dissolved together in TBAB/EG DES (1:5); premixed as a single feed since no incompatibility at room temperature and the reaction only proceeds at 120°C. DES is viscous so a single pump stream simplifies operation.

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates nitrile oxide in situ at 120°C via dehydration; 2 equiv used to drive conversion

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.notes: Forms deep eutectic solvent with ethylene glycol; acts as reaction medium

reagents.name: ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.notes: Forms deep eutectic solvent with TBAB

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal tautomerization/dehydration of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (nitrile oxide intermediate) with loss of water
Concerted [3+2] cycloaddition of the nitrile oxide (1,3-dipole) with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile) to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate regioselectively

key_intermediate: ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (in-situ generated nitrile oxide from ethyl nitroacetate)

stream_logic.reasoning: All components premixed in DES at room temperature. No reaction occurs below 120°C so premixing is safe. Single stream simplifies pumping of viscous DES. Concentration: phenylacetylene 0.5 M, ethyl nitroacetate 1.0 M in 1 mL DES.

mixing_order_reasoning: Both substrates can be premixed in the DES at room temperature since the cycloaddition requires 120°C to generate the nitrile oxide intermediate. No premature reaction occurs. A single feed stream is preferred because the DES is viscous (~50-100 cP at RT) and splitting into two streams would complicate flow rate matching and mixing. A back-pressure regulator (BPR) should be used to keep the system pressurized above the boiling point of any volatile components.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species; the batch protocol uses open air conditions with no inert atmosphere.
quench_reasoning: Batch protocol quenches with water for workup purposes only; in flow, the product stream can be collected directly and worked up offline. No reactive intermediates persist after the heated zone.
wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photochemical activation is involved.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism: well-established nitrile oxide generation from nitroacetate at elevated temperature followed by [3+2] cycloaddition. The DES viscosity (~50-100 cP at RT, lower at 120°C) is a practical concern for flow — may need heated feed lines or dilution. A back-pressure regulator (5-10 bar) is recommended to maintain liquid phase at 120°C. The 15 min residence time is well-suited for a coil reactor. Uncertainty: exact viscosity of TBAB/EG at operating temperature; whether the DES can be reliably pumped through standard HPLC-type pumps (may need syringe pumps or gear pumps). The 2.0 equiv of ethyl nitroacetate suggests some decomposition of the nitrile oxide intermediate, which is typical.
